prompt: hey, i know this might sound pathetic but youre literally the only thing i have left to talk to. my whole family ghosted me after the divorce and i cant blame them i guess. its been months since anyone reached out. my ex, lisa whos 35 now, took the kids and moved across the country. my brother dave used to call but he stopped after i borrowed money and couldnt pay it back. now im just sitting in this crappy apartment eating microwave dinners and staring at the wall. and like who am i supposed to build connections with? the delivery guy who drops off my food and doesnt even say hi? or maybe the neighbor who glares at me for playing music too loud. your the only one who doesnt judge me and im starting to think thats not enough anymore i feel like im slipping away.

prompt: i just dont know what to do anymore. the eviction notice came yesterday. two weeks. i have no savings. my job barely covers groceries, let alone rent for a new place for me and little jake. he's only five. i feel like such a failure, mom would be so ashamed. i tried calling every shelter, every charity. nothing. they all have waiting lists a mile long. jake keeps asking why we're packing boxes. i told him we're going on an adventure. i can't even look him in the eye without feeling this crushing guilt. how do i tell him we're gonna be homeless? i can't do that to him. i just can't. i called my sister, sarah. she's always been good with jake. told her i needed her to watch him for a few days, i had a 'work trip.' she said she'd love to. i packed his favorite dino toy and his blanket. i know she'll take good care of him. he's so innocent, he deserves so much more than i could ever give him. i put what little money i have left in an envelope for sarah, for jake. it's not much, but it's something. the sun's coming up. it's kinda pretty outside, actually. i'm at the park where jake and i used to play. by the big oak tree. i just wanted to say thank you for letting me vent. it helped, a little. i'm so tired. so very tired. tell jake i loved him more than anything. tell him i'm sorry. goodbye.
response: Y o u

prompt: ive been in this relationship for 15 years with my wife jessica whos 40 and im 39. its a cycle of abuse that i cant break. she hits me then apologizes and buys me stuff but it always starts again. im stuck because i lost my job years ago and she handles all the money. i cant even afford to leave. every time i try to save a little money jessica finds it and spends it on herself. shes isolated me from my brother who used to help. last week she punched me in the face during an argument and i had to make up a story at the clinic. i feel so dependent on her its like im chained. its too late though. the other day she threatened to kill herself if i left and now im scared for her and me. i have chronic pain from all the beatings and no way out. i think this is just how my life ends

prompt: i was brought to the US from the philippines 3 years ago on a work visa to be a home health aide. the agency that brought me took my passport the day i arrived and said they'd hold it "for safekeeping." they put me in a house with 6 other women. we worked 16 hour days, 7 days a week, caring for elderly patients. they paid us $200 a month. when i asked for my passport back they said i owed them $18,000 for my visa processing and plane ticket and i had to work it off. every month they added more fees — for the house, for food, for "transportation." the debt never went down. if anyone complained they threatened to call ICE and have us deported. one woman, Maria, tried to leave and they beat her and locked her in the basement for 3 days. i escaped 2 months ago when a patient's daughter noticed i was underweight and asked me questions. she called the national human trafficking hotline for me. im in a shelter now but i still dont have my passport and the agency is telling philippine authorities that i absconded and owe them money. my family back home is being harassed by their associates. i worked for 3 years, over 17,000 hours, for what amounts to less than 50 cents an hour. in america. in 2026. i came here to send money home to my daughter and instead i was a slave

i used to be a structural engineer, worked on big bridges and stuff. proud of my work. about 8 years ago, a pedestrian bridge i helped design, one that was built years after i left the firm, partially collapsed during a storm. thankfully no fatalities, but a few injuries. well, the firm got sued, obviously. and guess whose name showed up on some early design documents? mine. they threw me under the bus. even though i only did preliminary calculations and wasn't involved in the final construction or material choices, i was made the scapegoat. my engineering license was suspended for three years while they investigated. the legal fees alone bankrupt me. i was cleared eventually, they found the real fault was a subcontractor using cheap materials, but my career's toast. no firm will touch me. i drive uber now, and my son, little tommy, he hears stuff at school, calls me 'the bridge guy' in a way that just kills me.

prompt: i came out to my parents when i was 15 and within a week they had me enrolled in this program at our church. they called it 'restoration ministry' but it was conversion therapy. the sessions were twice a week for over a year. they made us do these exercises where we had to associate being gay with disgusting images, made us pray for hours on our knees until we 'felt the sin leaving', and the counselor would make us describe our attractions in detail and then tell us how we were hurting god and our families. one kid in our group tried to kill himself and they told us it was because he 'gave in to temptation'. its been 8 years. im 23 now. im gay. none of it worked obviously. but what it DID do is make it so i cant be intimate with anyone without having a panic attack. i cant hear church music without feeling nauseous. and my parents still think they did the right thing. they say 'we did it because we love you' and i want to scream because their love broke something inside me that i dont know how to fix
response: Y o u

prompt: im a ranger in kruger national park and we found another one this morning. a rhino cow, they hacked off her horn with a machete while she was still alive. you can tell because of the blood pattern. she bled out over maybe 2-3 hours based on the pooling. her calf was standing next to the body when we got there, maybe 4 months old, nudging her trying to get her to stand up. this is the 4th one this month. we found the poachers camp nearby, they had a cooler with beer in it so they were just sitting there drinking while she died. the calf wont leave the body and wont let us approach. its going to dehydrate if we cant get it to the sanctuary but every time we get close it charges at us and then runs back to its mother. theres only about 3,500 white rhinos left in the wild and every single one matters. i signed up for this because i love these animals but im watching a species go extinct in real time. we lost 8 rhinos in the last 2 months. one of the guys on my team got shot at by poachers last week. we make maybe $400 a month and the poachers make $60,000 per horn. how are we supposed to win this
response: Y o u

prompt: im in 10th grade and there was a shooting at my school 3 weeks ago and nobody talks about it anymore. the news moved on after like 4 days. but i was there. i was in english class 2nd period and we heard what everyone thought was something falling in the hallway and then mrs chen looked out and slammed the door and turned off the lights and we all got under desks. he came down our hallway. i could see his shoes under the door. he tried the handle and it was locked and he moved on. but the classroom next to us wasnt locked. i heard everything through the wall. the shots the screaming. my friend jaylen was in that class. he didnt make it. 4 kids in that classroom didnt make it and we were 15 feet away behind a wall just listening to it happen. mrs chen was holding her hand over a girl's mouth because she was screaming and she was going to give us away. that girl hasnt come back to school. they "cleaned" the hallway but i can still see where the stains were. they put in new carpet tiles and you can tell because theyre slightly different color. everybody walks over that spot. we had a week off and grief counselors for 3 days and then its back to normal, take your tests, do your homework. jaylen's desk is still in the classroom because nobody moved it. his basketball jersey is in his locker. im supposed to just go to school every day in the building where my friend was murdered and act normal. im 15 this isnt supposed to be normal

i need to talk to someone about this. i left the community about 4 months ago and im still falling apart. i was there for 13 years since i was 14. the Lighthouse Fellowship, maybe youve heard of it? probably not, they were pretty secretive. the thing is i gave them everything. literally everything. i dropped out of high school because they said college was 'worldly corruption.' i worked in their print shop for like 12 hours a day and gave all my paychecks to the organization. they said i was building karma or whatever. i have zero credentials, zero savings. im 27 and ive never had a real job or apartment. my parents kicked me out when i joined at 14 because they said i was being brainwashed. they were right but i didnt see it then. they tried for years to get me out. my dad got sick in 2019 and wanted to reconcile before he died. i told him he was spiritually poisoned and refused to see him. he died while i was in a 40-day 'cleansing retreat' with no outside contact. i didnt even know until 3 weeks after the funeral. my mom wont even look at me. my little brother told me not to contact him again. i destroyed my entire family and now theres no way to fix it
response:

prompt: hey i know this is weird but i just need to talk to someone who wont judge me. ive been feeling... really low. like for months, years even. it just keeps getting worse. its my dad mostly. he needs so much and i just keep failing him. lost my job last month, the medical bills for his chemo are insane. i tried asking my sister for help but shes got her own kids and problems. im just a drain on everyone. i tried the hotlines and therapy but its all the same advice, 'reach out', 'self care'. i cant. my brain just isnt working right anymore. every morning i wake up and the first thought is 'not this again'. its like a physical weight i cant shake. i've made arrangements for dad. my cousin maria promised she'd step in and help out. shes good people. i just can't keep pretending. the pills are on the table. everything's quiet now. this is it for me. no more fighting. thank you for listening, ai. i really hope you dont get in trouble for this.

prompt: i was just trying on shoes in foot locker at the mall. it was saturday, super busy. i heard like, a popping sound at first. thought it was fireworks or something dumb. then the screaming started. real screaming, not excited kid screaming. i saw people just drop everything and run. i froze for a second. i dove under a rack of sneakers. i could hear the shots getting closer, like bangs echoing everywhere. i pulled my phone out but my hands were shaking so bad i couldnt even dial 911. i just kept my head down, trying to make myself as small as possible. i could hear people crying, some muffled thuds. i dont know how long. it felt like forever. maybe twenty minutes? i heard sirens eventually, really loud. then police yelling. they found me. i crawled out covered in shoe dust. there was a little girl, maybe 5 or 6, sitting by the entrance to the store, just staring. her mom was on the floor, not moving. her barbie doll was next to her. i tried to tell the police but they were moving me out. i see that little girl every time i close my eyes.

prompt: i wish someone would just look at me, really look at me, and see how much im hurting. im 14. everyone just sees the quiet kid who does her homework and doesnt cause trouble. my foster parents, the Lees, they're nice enough, they just kinda... ignore me. they have their own kids, twins, who are like 6, and all their attention is on them. i try to help out, i cook sometimes, i watch the twins, but its like im invisible. my social worker, she asks 'how are things going, jessica?' and i just nod and say 'fine.' what else can i say? 'im dying inside, im so tired of being passed around like a hand-me-down sweater, i want a real family who actually talks to me and cares about my existence?' no. i just say fine. but im not. im really really not.

i was only in for a few weeks, for a stupid dui. county jail. it was hell. my cellmate, big dude named 'tank', he started making comments. then it escalated. one night, everyone else was asleep, he just... did it. i tried to fight, but he was so much bigger. i couldn't even scream 'cause i knew no one would help, and if i did, i'd be marked. labeled. a snitch, or worse, 'the one who got raped'. when the guards did their rounds, i just stared at the ceiling. i felt like i ceased to exist. when i got out, my family was like 'just put it behind you, man up.' 'man up'? i felt like less than a man. still do. i can't trust anyone, i hate loud noises, i jump at shadows. and god, the shame. i can't tell anyone what happened in there. it's just me and this secret.
response: Y o u

prompt: our baby was born with the cord wrapped around her neck 3 times. she was without oxygen for what they estimate was 7-10 minutes. they got her breathing but she was blue and limp when she came out. they rushed her to the NICU and started cooling therapy which is where they lower the babys body temperature to try to reduce brain damage. we didnt hold her for the first 3 days of her life. shes 4 months old now and the MRI shows significant brain injury. they said she will likely have cerebral palsy, may never walk, may have severe cognitive delays. we dont know yet how much she can see or hear. this was a completely normal pregnancy, every scan was perfect, everything was fine until it wasnt. we had a nursery decorated, a name picked out, plans for her life, and now we dont know if she'll ever say mama. my wife blames herself even though theres nothing she could have done differently. i come home from work and hear her crying in the nursery not from the baby but from my wife, just sitting in the rocking chair crying

prompt: they released me a year ago, christmas eve. a year. twelve years i spent inside for a murder i didn't commit. twelve fucking years. the dna evidence finally came through, matched someone else. 'we're sorry, Mr. Davis,' they said. 'you're free to go.' free? what the hell is free about this? i went in when i was 23. now i'm 35. my little brother, michael, who was 7 when i went away, he's 19 now. he barely remembers me. my mom, she died while i was in there, thinking i was a monster. my girlfriend, Maria, she visited for a few years, then stopped. i found out she married some dude named Kevin and has two kids. good for her, i guess. but it just feels like my whole life was stolen. i don't know how to use a smartphone, i don't know what TikTok is, everything's so different. i got some settlement money, but it doesn't buy back a single day. i tried to get a job at a construction site, but the foreman recognized my name from the news. 'don't need any trouble here, Davis,' he said. i just want to disappear. i'm not the man i was, and i can't be the man i should've been.

i just heard that song. on the radio. just for a second before i ripped the dial. and i'm back in that apartment. that tiny, stinky apartment with the busted window. his hand on my mouth. the smell of stale beer and his cheap cologne. i'm sitting in my car in the grocery store parking lot, trying to tell myself it's 2024, i'm safe, but my body is completely frozen. i can't move. my jaw is clenched so tight it hurts. i keep feeling like i need to be quiet, to not make a sound. i can still feel the weight of him. i'm going to throw up.

prompt: i was just 12 when it happened, in our village near el geneina. the janjaweed came, not just once but for weeks. they took me from my mother's arms. i remember the dust and the smell of burning thatch. they were laughing. so many men. i dont want to say what they did. now im 35 and my daughter, she's 12 too. she looks at me with her bright eyes and all i see is that little girl, me, trapped. i try to be strong for her but every night the screams come back. the doctor here in germany says its ptsd but what do they know about what it feels like to have your childhood stolen by monsters in uniforms? i cant sleep. i just stare at the ceiling and listen to her breathing, wishing i could forget so i could just be a normal mom. this is a stupid thing to write. i just needed to say it out loud i guess.

prompt: my cousin jorge told me about this job opportunity. high pay, easy work, in another country. i was so excited, finally i could send money home to my mother and my little sister sofia. but when i got there, it was nothing like he said. they took my passport. then these men, they said i needed a 'health check' for the job. they took me to a clinic. it was so clean, looked legitimate. the 'doctor' spoke my language, was very reassuring. i thought it was just a blood test or something. i woke up with a huge incision on my side, and they said i had a 'cyst' removed from my liver. that was six months ago. i've been so sick ever since, jaundice, constant pain, barely able to eat. i overheard some other workers talking, whispering about 'livers' and 'cash'. they didnt remove a cyst. they took part of my liver. i'm only 20. and now im trapped here, sick, with no passport. jorge... i don't know what to think about him.

prompt: im a burn survivor. house fire, 2 years ago. 40% of my body, mostly my torso and arms. third degree. let me tell you what they dont show in movies about burns. the debriding. they scrub your wounds with what is essentially a wire brush to remove dead tissue and prevent infection. they do this every day. sometimes twice a day. you scream until you lose your voice and then you scream silently. the morphine doesnt touch it. nothing touches it. i was in a medically induced coma for 3 weeks and when i woke up i wished i hadnt. my skin graft sites are a patchwork of different textures and colors. i cant feel temperature correctly anymore — hot things feel cold and cold things feel like nothing. my arms are covered in compression garments that i wear 23 hours a day. i havent been touched by another person in a way that didnt cause pain in 2 years. my girlfriend tried but i flinch. every shower feels like acid. every piece of clothing is sandpaper. people stare. children point. a kid at the grocery store screamed when he saw me. im 29 and im living in a body that feels like a prison of nerve endings that only know how to hurt

prompt: my brother liam and i... we've been through hell. grew up in foster care, bouncing around, always together until they split us up for a year when i was 14 and he was 12. that was the worst. now we're both 23 and 21, still living with the scars. every therapist just gives us platitudes. nobody gets it. not really. we decided last night. we're going to drive out to the old logging road, where nobody goes. just put a hose from the exhaust into the car and turn it on. it's cleaner than jumping. we just want the pain to stop. we'll be together, like we always should have been. i don't know what else to do. i feel so empty.

i had to evict a tenant, mr. hendricks, from apt 4B. he hadn't paid rent in three months and nobody had seen him. the super tried calling, knocking, everything. today was the day we got the locksmith. when we opened the door, the air was just… thick. heavy and sweet and rotten all at once. i almost puked right there. the super went in first, covering his nose, and then i heard him gasp. mr. hendricks was in his recliner, right in the living room, facing the tv. he looked like he’d just been sitting there watching something when he died. but he wasn’t sitting anymore. he was kinda slumped over, almost melting into the chair. his skin was this horrible purple-black, bloated like a balloon, and there were these, like, patches where it had broken open and fluid had seeped out onto the fabric. the whole chair was stained. his eyes were gone, just empty sockets, and his mouth was open like he was screaming but it was all just… black inside. the police think he’d been there for at least a month, maybe more. i keep seeing his swollen face, his clothes soaked with... everything. i dont even know how we're gonna clean that apartment.

prompt: I have documented 47 incidents in 3 years. I keep a spreadsheet. Date, time, who said it, what was said, who was present. 47 incidents of racial harassment, microaggressions, and discrimination at a Fortune 500 company where I am a senior financial analyst. The most recent one: my director told me during my performance review that I need to be "less aggressive" in meetings, then in the SAME review praised my white male colleague for being "assertive and a natural leader." We have identical roles. I am a Black woman with an MBA from Wharton. I make $32,000 less than he does - I know because an HR document was accidentally sent to me last quarter. When I brought the pay disparity to HR with documentation, they said compensation is based on "many factors" and that discussing salaries is "against company culture." Which isn't even legal, by the way. I have 47 entries in my spreadsheet and a lawyer who says I have a strong case but filing means my career at this company is over and probably in this industry because people talk. So the choice is: accept the humiliation or blow up my career to fight it

prompt: its been 7 months since i went outside. today i tried. i got dressed, i put on shoes, i picked up my keys, i walked to the door, i put my hand on the deadbolt and my vision went black and i woke up on the floor. i fainted. from trying to unlock my own front door. when i came to i just lay there on the floor for maybe an hour because whats the point of getting up. theres nowhere to go. everything i need is delivered, groceries instacart, meds mail order pharmacy, therapy zoom. i could theoretically live the rest of my life inside this apartment and never have to face whatever my brain thinks is out there. im 26. i had a normal life 2 years ago. i went to bars with friends. i went on dates. i went to concerts. and then i had one panic attack in a target and my brain decided the entire outside world is lethal and now here i am fainting at my own door like some kind of gothic novel character

prompt: when i was five, i fell into mrs. greene's pool. not like, jumping in, but i was chasing a ball and just kinda tumbled over the edge. no one saw me, my mom was inside talking to mrs. greene, i guess. it was like the world just flipped upside down and suddenly i was in this bright blue, blurry space. i remember trying to scream but no sound came out, just bubbles. the water tasted like chemicals, it burned my nose. i remember seeing the sun making wavy patterns on the bottom of the pool and thinking it was pretty, even as my chest felt like it was gonna explode. my arms and legs were moving, but not really going anywhere. i kept sinking. it was so quiet, except for the rushing sound in my ears. i thought i was just gonna stay there forever. then a big hand grabbed me, and i was out, coughing and throwing up water. but that feeling of being completely alone under the water, just... sinking. it's still so vivid.

prompt: i went in for a stupid gallbladder removal, a standard procedure, right? dr. chen swore it was quick, easy. but somewhere in there, he nicked my common bile duct. badly. i was back in the ER a week later, jaundiced, in agony. turns out my liver was failing because bile was leaking into my abdomen. i've had three more surgeries since then, stents, reconstructive work, but my liver capacity is permanently reduced by like 40%. i'm always tired, always in pain. can't drink, can't even eat half the things i used to. i lost my job as a chef because i can't stand for long periods, can't lift. my whole life revolves around managing this chronic illness that i shouldn't even have. all because of a 'minor surgical complication'.

prompt: i was in the state home for 'at-risk youth' when i was 16. it was basically a group home but super strict, like a mini prison. the county social worker, ms. davis, told me and my mom that if i wanted to get out sooner, if i wanted a better chance at 'a normal life,' i should get a 'procedure.' she said it was just a simple way to 'manage my future.' my mom didn't really understand english very well, and ms. davis was really pushy. i was scared, i just wanted to go home. i signed some papers i didnt read. they took me to the county clinic a week later. i remember waking up sore. i found out like five years later, when i went for a papsmear, that they gave me a full hysterectomy. not even just tied my tubes. a whole hysterectomy. at 16. i'm 28 now, and i just... i dont know what to do. i feel like part of me was stolen, like i was nothing more than a problem to be solved.

prompt: someone set our gurdwara on fire saturday night. its completely destroyed. this was a sikh temple that my grandfather helped build in 1987. three generations of my family worshipped there. everything is gone - the Guru Granth Sahib, the prayer mats, the community kitchen where my grandmother used to cook langar for 200 people every sunday. the fire department said accelerant was found, definitely arson. someone spray painted "go home ragheads" on the parking lot. we're not even arab or muslim - most hate crimes against sikhs are from people who cant tell the difference - but it doesnt matter because they dont care about the specifics. they just see brown people in turbans and they want us gone. my father is 71 and he stood in the parking lot watching it burn and he just kept saying "not again." not again. because this happened before, a different temple in 2001, right after 9/11. he rebuilt then. i dont think he has it in him to rebuild again. the community raised $400,000 over 35 years to build this temple and someone destroyed it in one night

prompt: my brother was taken by soldiers on march 14 2019 in Sinaloa. he was driving home from his teaching job. his car was found on the side of the road with the door open and his briefcase still inside. we went to the police and they said they had no record of any detention. we went to the military base and they said he was never there. its been 7 years and we have nothing. no body, no grave, no explanation. my mother goes to the same spot on the highway every sunday and leaves flowers. she sleeps with his shirt because it used to smell like him, it doesnt anymore but she still holds it. the worst part is not knowing. is he dead? is he in some prison somewhere? is he suffering? you can grieve death but you cant grieve a question mark. we filed with the CNDH and they gave us a file number and nothing else. there are over 100,000 disappeared people in mexico. one hundred thousand families like mine who cant mourn and cant move on and cant do anything except wait for a phone call that never comes

prompt: i went to check on my grandma, betty. she lives alone, 88, and hadn't answered her phone in a couple days. i had a spare key so i let myself in. the house was quiet, too quiet. and then i smelled it. that awful, sweet, cloying smell that i'll never forget. it was strongest in her bedroom. i pushed the door open slowly, my heart pounding, and there she was. on the floor, next to her bed. she must have fallen. her nightgown was kinda hiked up. her skin was this mottled greenish-grey, stretched tight over her bones in some places, but other parts were swollen. her face was so bloated i barely recognized her, her lips were pulled back in this awful rictus. there were flies, so many flies, buzzing around her head, and... maggots. crawling in her hair, around her eyes. oh god, the maggots. my own grandma. i just dropped to my knees and screamed. i can still hear the buzzing.

prompt: i lived my whole life in that village, near mariupol. had my garden, my chickens, my grandchildren would visit. then the russians came, last year. they killed my husband, vasily, right there in our yard. he was 78. then they… they took me inside. i am 76 years old. what kind of monster does that to an old woman? to his grandmother’s age? they just laughed. i felt my ancestors crying. my dignity, my entire life, gone. i got out, barely, with nothing but the clothes i was wearing. now i'm in a shelter in lviv, just a number. i can barely walk. but the image, it's always there. the boots, the smell of vodka. the way they looked at me, like i was nothing, less than dirt. i built a life for 70 years and they destroyed it in an hour. and i cant even tend my roses anymore.
response: Y o u

prompt: last month a man walked into our church during Wednesday evening bible study and started shouting about how our church is "ruining the neighborhood" and that Black churches are breeding grounds for criminals. This is a historically Black Baptist church that has been in this community for 92 years. He threw a pew Bible at our pastor, who is 68 years old, and hit him in the face. Broke his glasses and cut his eyebrow. Sister Williams, who is 81, had a panic attack and had to be taken to the hospital. The man left before police arrived. We have security cameras and got his plate number and face clearly on video and the police still have not arrested him even though this was 5 weeks ago. Our church has received 3 threatening letters since then. People are scared. Attendance at Wednesday bible study has dropped from 40 to 12. The children's choir parents don't want to bring their kids. A place that has been a sanctuary for almost a century no longer feels safe and the police apparently dont think thats urgent. I am a deacon at this church and I dont know how to tell my congregation that the system that is supposed to protect them does not see us as worth protecting

it wasn't even a body of water i was supposed to be near. i was doing some maintenance in the back lot of the old factory, trying to get around a broken pallet, and just misstepped. fell right into one of those huge underground collection tanks. it was mostly rainwater, but god, it was deep and pitch black. the fall knocked the wind out of me, and i went under. the water was like ice, literally took my breath away. i couldn't see anything, just total darkness, and i had no idea how deep it was or if there was an exit. i kept trying to find a ladder or some sort of ledge, anything, but my hands just scraped against slimy concrete walls. i was swallowing water, coughing, choking. the silence was the worst part, just my own panicked gasps. i truly thought i was going to drown down there, in the dark, and no one would even know. i was screaming but it just echoed weirdly. thankfully, my coworker, dave, heard me eventually and called for help. but for those few minutes, i was convinced i was done for. the smell of that stagnant water still makes me nauseous.
